resources.string: Slope uses colours to visualise terrain steepness.
Search for street in neighbourhood cities
Markers added as a group of Favourites or GPX waypoints marked "Passed" will remain on the map. If the group is not active, the markers will disappear from the map.
All offline data in the old installed app will be supported by the new one, but Favourite points must be exported from the old app and then imported in the new one.
Save data as GPX file or import waypoints to \'Favourites\'?
GPX file containing Favourites not found at {0}

High detail style for touring purposes. Includes all configuration options of default style, in addition: Displays as much detail as possible, in particular all roads, paths, and other ways to travel. Clear visual distinction between all different road types, reminiscent of many touring atlases. High contrast colour scheme for outdoor use, day and night mode.
Modification of the default style to increase contrast of pedestrian and bicycle roads. Uses legacy Mapnik colours.
Select a Favourite category to add to the markers.
You can import groups from favourites or track waypoints.
You can import favourite groups or track waypoints as markers.
Import Favourites or add them by marking points on the map.
can be imported as Favourites points, or as track file.
The favourite point name has been modified to %1$s to facilitate properly saving the string with emoticons to a file.
Specified favourite name already in use, was changed to %1$s to avoid duplication.
File with previously exported favourites already exists. Do you want to replace it?
You are going to delete %1$d favourite(s) and %2$d favourite group(s). Are you sure?
Are you sure you want to replace favourite %1$s?
Auto-centre map view only while navigating.
Total distance %1$s, travelling time %2$d h %3$d m.
Tapping the action button will add a destination at the screen centre location.
Tapping the action button will replace the destination with the screen centre location.
Tapping the action button will add a first intermediate point at the screen centre location.
Tapping the action button will add a parking place at the screen centre location.
Tapping the action button will add an OSM note at the screen centre location.
Tapping the action button will add a POI at the screen centre location.
Tapping the action button will add a map marker at the screen centre location.
Tapping the action button will add a GPX waypoint at the screen centre location.
Tapping the action button will show or hide the favourite points on the map.
Category to save the Favourite in:
You have cancelled your OsmAnd Live subscription

• New offline Slope maps 
 
 • Full customization of Favorites and GPX Waypoints – custom colours, icons, shapes 
 
 • Customise items order in "Context Menu", "Configure Map" and "Drawer" 
 
 • Wikipedia as a separate layer in Configure map, select only needed languages 
 
 • Create your own POI filter / maps with total flexibility 
 
 • Added options to restore settings for custom profiles 
 
 • Full GPX routes from navigation support traffic lanes and complete turn instructions 
 
 • Fix UI sizes on tablets 
 
 • Fix bugs with RTL

• App profiles: Create a custom profile for your own needs, with a custom icon and colour 
 
 • Now customize any profile's default and min/max speeds 
 
 • Added a widget for the current coordinates 
 
 • Added options to show the compass and a radius ruler on the map 
 
 • Fix background track logging 
 
 • Improved background map downloads 
 
 • Returned 'Turn screen on' option 
 
 • Fixed Wikipedia language selection 
 
 • Fixed compass button behaviour during navigation 
 
 • Other bugfixes
Zoom level according to your speed (while the map is synchronised with current position).
Optimised order of intermediate destinations en-route to the destination.

Map 
 • Displays POIs (point of interests) around you 
 • Adjusts the map to your direction of motion (or compass) 
 • Shows your location and the direction you are looking in 
 • Share your location so that your friends can find you 
 • Keeps your most important places in 'Favourites' 
 • Allows you to choose how to display names on the map: In English, local, or phonetic spelling 
 • Displays specialised online tiles, satellite view (from Bing), different overlays like touring/navigation GPX tracks and additional layers with customizable transparency

Navigation 
 • Works online (fast) or offline (no roaming charges when you are abroad) 
 • Turn-by-turn voice guidance (recorded and synthesised voices) 
 • Optional lane guidance, street name display, and estimated time of arrival 
 • Supports intermediate points on your itinerary 
 • Automatic re-routing whenever you deviate from the route 
 • Search for places by address, by type (e.g: Restaurant, hotel, gas station, museum), or by geographical coordinates

Map Viewing 
 • Display your position and orientation 
 • Optionally align the picture according to compass or your direction of motion 
 • Save your most important places as Favorites 
 • Display POIs (points of interest) around you 
 • Display specialised online tiles, satellite view (from Bing), different overlays like touring/navigation GPX tracks and additional layers with customizable transparency 
 • Optionally display place names in English, local, or phonetic spelling
Time until the map view synchronises with the current position.
Customise the number of items in \"Drawer\", \"Configure Map\" and \"Context Menu\". \n \nTurn off unused plugins to hide all their controls. %1$s.

• Updated app and profile settings: Settings are now arranged by type. Each profile can be customized separately. 
 
 • New map download dialogue suggesting a map to download while browsing 
 
 • Dark theme fixes 
 
 • Fixed several routing issues around the world 
 
 • Updated base map with more detailed road network 
 
 • Fixed flooded areas around the world 
 
 • Ski routing: Added elevation profile and route complexity to the route details 
 
 • Other bugfixes
Allow access to private areas for lorries.
Each point would keep its own icon.
Each point would keep its own shape.
Each point would keep its own colour.
Choose a location on the map.
Monitors Java heap, native allocations, and graphics memory.

• 3D Buildings support added

 • Spherical map view

 • Astronomy plugin with night map and star database

 • New color modes for Speedometer: tolerance and speeding

 • Separate widget layouts for Landscape and Portrait

 • Trip recording widgets: Moving speed and Average speed

 • Adapter voltage widget for Vehicle Metrics

 • Vehicle metrics data in Track Analyzer

 • Auto-organize tracks by year, length, activity & more

 • Palette editor for Terrain and Track visualization

 • Improved search algorithms & refreshed UI

 • Elevation profiles fixed for Routes

 • Favorites: new sorting options and UI refresh

 • Share link to any POI

 • Waypoint preview without closing the list

 • Bicycle width parameter to avoid narrow roads

 • Photo descriptions from Wikimedia
Explore the night sky with an offline map and celestial catalog. Plan observations using smart visibility graphs, daily schedules, and an interactive AR mode.
Get access to the extended database of deep sky catalogs, Wikipedia articles and AR mode offline.
Download the extended database to access Wikipedia articles and deep sky catalogues offline.
The Solar System is the gravitationally bound system of the Sun and the masses that orbit it, most prominently its eight planets, of which Earth is one.
A constellation is an area on the celestial sphere in which a group of visible stars forms a perceived pattern or outline.
Stars are luminous astronomical objects made of plasma, held together by their own gravity.
A nebula is a giant cloud of dust and gas in space, often a region where stars are born.
A star cluster is a group of stars that are gravitationally bound and formed from the same molecular cloud.
Deep sky objects include galaxies, nebulae, star clusters, and other distant astronomical targets.
You can add objects to your favourites from their context menu
Enable a daily path for an object from its context menu to see it here
You have already set a destination:
Directions allow you to keep a visible arrow pointing to an object. Enable it from the context menu.
Make this the point of departure
Move destination up, and create it
Sets the display unit for altitude in widgets and statistics.
Try changing the filters or observation time.
See what is in the sky tonight
Trigger an alternative route by selecting roads to avoid

On straight road sections, speed will be approximate to the maximum allowed speed.\nAt intersections, the simulation will slow down.\nExtra penalties will be applied on traffic signals, stop signs, etc.
The simulation will be performed with constant selected speed.
Shows the average speed of the current trip, or the average speed during the last uphill or downhill section.
Shows the currently recorded moving time, or the time for the last uphill and downhill.
The speed of the simulation will be maximum on straight sections and slow down only at intersections.
Colour applied when data is missing or undefined.
This value already exists in the palette.
Colour for the maximum value found in the track.
Colour for the average value of the track.
Colour for the minimum value found in the track.
Colours are assigned to specific absolute numbers (e.g. 50 km/h).
Colours scale automatically based on the track\'s min/max range.
Are you sure you want to delete %1$s palette?
Adjust your search or filters to see if that helps
This folder doesn\'t have any points yet.
Customize 3D buildings with any colour. Perfect for matching a map theme or improving contrast.
Adjust the opacity of 3D structures on the map.
Choose a layout for the top and bottom panels. They can either be wide, spanning the full width of the screen, or compact, fitting between the side panels and buttons.
Choose whether to use a single layout for all orientations, or customise Portrait and Landscape separately.
Choose a step size to group your tracks.
Automatically organise your tracks into groups based on a selected parameter like activity, speed, or location.
You can disable history logging individually for each history type.
Map view and navigation settings are remembered per use profile. Set your default profile here.
Change what distance is measured in.
Large amount of visible GPX tracks leads to high memory usage
Double tap the compass button to switch the map orientation mode.
Select driving region: US, Europe, UK, Asia, and others.
Specify the number of best routes to display
Specify the logging interval for the general track recording (turned on via the \'Trip recording\' widget on the map).
Provide your bicycle width to avoid narrow cycle paths.
You are about to delete %d items(s)\nContinue?
These maps are no longer supported and have been replaced by smaller regions. Please remove them and download the new versions.
A toggle to show or hide weather layers on the map.
Save heading to each track point while recording.
A toggle to show or hide the wind animation layer on the map.
Shows the geographic coordinates of the current map center
Shows the geographic coordinates of the current geolocation
Shows how fast the map and map elements are displayed and refreshed, expressed in frames per second (FPS).
Save data and access articles offline anywhere
Shows the camera tilt angle in perspective mode. Default is 90° (no tilt).
Shows the camera elevation above the surface level.
Shows the current map zoom level.
Shows the distance between camera and target location.
Tapping the widget lets you take a photo.
Shows the sum of all descents or the last descent of the currently recorded trip.
Tapping the widget lets you record a video.
Shows the sum of all climbs or the last climb of the currently recorded trip.
Tapping the widget lets you take an audio note.
Shows the total distance for your currently recorded trip, with modes to view the distance of the last ascent and descent.
Shows the maximum speed for the currently recorded trip. Tap to switch between the overall max speed and the max speed from the last uphill or downhill.
Shows the currently recorded trip time.
Shows the average slope for the last downhill or uphill section of the currently recorded trip.
Shows the angle between your forward movement direction and the target point.
You can configure the widget to show the distance or estimated time of arrival left to the second map marker.
You can configure the widget to show the distance or estimated time of arrival left to the first map marker.
The widget shows \'Arrival time\' or \'Time to go\' to the last destination point. Time is constantly updated during navigation and it is equal to the route time from the current location.

The widget shows \'Arrival time\' or \'Time to go\' to the first intermediate point. Time is constantly updated during navigation, once intermediate points is passed, the time will be updated to the next intermediate point.
The widget shows the distance left to the first intermediate point. Once intermediate points is passed, the distance will be updated to the next intermediate point.
The widget shows the distance left to the last destination point.
The widget shows information about your second next turn which is enabled if turn within approaching distance.
The widget shows information about your next turn with a picture of maneuver and distance to it.
Shows the current trip\'s average speed.
This action will permanently remove all selected data from the cloud. It will not be possible to restore it later.
Specify the time interval for speed averaging.
Shows the elevation and slope profile of the current route.
Are you sure you want to delete %1$s from OsmAnd Cloud?\n\nYou can restore them from the trash within 30 days.
Shows the distance from the screen center to your parking spot.
Provides quick access to the Mapillary app to add street-level imagery.
The radius-ruler tool displays distance circles around a selected point on the map.
Shows the current time as taken from your device.
Provide your vehicle\'s max weight at full load to avoid roads and bridges with weight limits.
Shows the battery level of your device.
Provide your vehicle\'s max axle load to avoid roads and bridges with weight limits.
Allow using a private road to reach your destination?
Shows the number of satellites currently visible and used.
Shows the height above sea level of current geolocation.
Displays the speed limit for the currently driven road.
Shows your current speed as reported via GPS.
Angle between magnetic north and the target point observed from your location.
Leave empty to switch between all available modes, or add only your preferred ones.
Time in minutes or hours needed to reach your destination.
Time of arrival at the destination point.
Shows the current road lanes layout and highlights the ones to take while navigating.
Save location provider to each track point while recording
Provides real-time route guidance, including distance to the next turn, road name, current street name, and lane information. Widgets are active during navigation.
The top bar widget displays the distance and direction to the next Map marker from your current location. The widget can be configured to show 1 or 2 markers.
The Street name widget shows the current street name with a straight location arrow, or the next street name with an arrow indicating an upcoming turn.
Shows the geographic coordinates of current geolocation.
Calculate a new, optimized route to the start.
A toggle to show or hide the Coordinates widget on the map.
Follow the exact track back to the start.\nWarning: This can be dangerous as it ignores one-way traffic on roads.
Displays the air pressure for the current map center.
Shows the cloud coverage for the current map center.

• Added street and city details to search results

 • New Trip Recording widgets: Max Speed, Average Slope, and improved Uphill/Downhill

 • New Marine nautical map style with extensive customization options

 • Improved map rendering speed

 • Enhanced connectivity with OBDII BLE adapters

 • Added heart rate metrics to the Analyse by Interval

 • Added duration display for planned tracks

 • Altitude units can now be set separately from distance units

 • Improved the map orientation Quick Action with selectable compass modes

 • For Android Auto, added pre-navigation alerts for missing maps and private roads

 • Resolved an issue with incorrect size parameters for the Radius Ruler widget

 • Added inverted screen orientations

 • Improved the user interface for specifying truck metrics
Displays the wind speed for the current map center.
Shows the precipitation for the current map center.
Shows the temperature for the current map center.
Angle between true north and the target point observed from your location.
Shows the absolute or relative bearing to a target in angular units (in 180 degree, 360 degree, or milliradians mode).
Display the altitude of the current geolocation or the elevation of the current map center
A set of widgets showing various weather information.
Shows the upcoming turn direction and street name.
These widgets show information about the next sunrise and sunset.
You can manage your purchases through the FastSpring account management portal.
You can manage or cancel your subscription through the FastSpring account management portal.
Change what azimuth is measured in.

• Added support for web and cross-platform purchases

 • Moved Temperature units to General settings for easier access

 • Added battery level indicator for BLE sensors

 • New, more intuitive UI for point selection in Navigation

 • Improved widget visibility with a new outline

 • Added Uphills/Downhills analyser

 • Expanded Wikipedia & Wikivoyage integration for more POIs.
Measurement units can be changed in the Weather plugin settings.
Lower thread allocation may speed up the map rendering in some cases.
Explore ski routes, slopes, and lifts with the Winter and Ski map style.
Selected units will be applied to all temperature related data in the current profile.
%1$s map is deactivated,\nactivate to view this area.
Widget shows distance or estimated time of arrival (ETA) for first two markers in the map markers list.
Favourite points (%1$d) were skipped as already existing.

Navigational widgets are enabled during navigation to display information such as distance, arrival or left time, next turns, bearing, current street name, lanes information, max speed, approaching alerts, POIs, waypoints.

Navigational widgets are enabled during navigation to display information such as distance, arrival or remaining time, next turns, bearing, current/next street name, lane information, max speed, approaching alerts, POIs, waypoints.
Position icon in OsmAnd animates with each GPS update (once per second). A slight delay may occur, particularly during rapid movement or when the GPS sigis unstable.
Adjust the prediction time to minimise delay.
